Description: We are seeking a seasoned Senior Business Development Manager to join one of the most impactful teams at Anomalo. This role focuses on developing and executing co-sell strategies with major technology partners to drive annual recurring revenue and enhance Anomalo's market presence. This person will be tasked with having an in-depth understanding of the sales and product organizations at some of our most strategic partners, such as Snowflake and Databricks. They will be the primary point of contact for all partnership activities, enabling them on Anomalo’s product and establishing a mutual value proposition for the partnership across go-to-market and product teams. The role requires an individual with the experience, interpersonal skills, and energy to operate in a wide spectrum of situations. They will be an enthusiastic and motivated individual who is visibly passionate and can attract and form lasting relationships. This position will report to the Director of Business Development. What you’ll do as the Senior Business Development Manager at Anomalo: Effectively manage partner relationships, developing rapport with key decision makers, and serve as a primary point of contact for all partnership activities Identify and support GTM opportunities with partners by aligning with their goals and roadmaps to drive Anomalo’s overall strategy and expansion goals Drive ARR growth through effective partnership frameworks-including developing comprehensive co-sell strategies that align with both Anomalo's and partners' goals Work Cross-Functionally with Anomalo marketing, sales, and customer success teams to ensure that GTM strategies are integrated across all aspects of Anomalo's operations Understand partner and Anomalo roadmaps to identify net new points of integration, and work with both Anomalo and the partner’s product and engineering teams on scoping ROI and seeing these to fruition What you'll bring to the team: Over 8 years of professional experience, with 5+ years in partner or account development in the software/technology industry Experience managing joint GTM activities with global companies, including development and tracking of joint sell-with programs Knowledge of the Modern Data Stack and experience with platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Google Strong written, oral and social communication skills Focus on execution Experience working cross-functionally is required Strong ability to influence without direct control Ideal candidates will also have: Experience owning partnerships with Snowflake or Databricks or hyperscalers such as Google Enterprise sales experience Salary range: The estimated on-target earnings for this role is $200,000 - $220,000, plus meaningful equity Travel: Planned travel once per quarter to spend time with the team is required Ability to travel (up to 25%) in support of the above responsibilities

About Boomi and What Makes Us Special

Are you ready to work at a fast-growing company where you can make a difference? Boomi aims to make the world a better place by connecting everyone to everything, anywhere. Our award-winning, intelligent integration and automation platform helps organizations power the future of business. At Boomi, you’ll work with world-class people and industry-leading technology. We hire trailblazers with an entrepreneurial spirit who can solve challenging problems, make a real impact, and want to be part of building something big. If this sounds like a good fit for you, check out boomi.com  or visit our Boomi Careers page to learn more.

How You’ll Make An Impact

As an AI Forward Deployed Engineer, you will be the hands-on expert responsible for turning a customer's agentic vision into a reality. In this critical role, you will engage with the customer team to ideate use cases and create demos/POCs.  Assisting the Sales & COE team, you will scope the platform work and partner with Professional Services to scope the implementation work.

Post-Sale, your mission is to execute the Agentic "Solution Blueprint" defined during the sales cycle, ensuring customers achieve their first major value milestone as quickly and efficiently as possible.

What You’ll Do

  • Identify Use Cases: Work with the presales SC to identify and refine future use cases for each customer based on implementation insights.

  • Create Demos / POCs: Work with the customer team to identify key proof points and build out a demo/POC that successfully shows how the Boomi platform delivers.

  • Lead Implementation: Develop a detailed project plan for the customer implementation, based on the agent's predefined steps and the Solution Blueprint.

  • Provide Hands-on Expertise: Act as the primary technical point of contact during onboarding, providing hands-on guidance and project management for the setup, configuration, and integration required by the agent.

  • Drive to First Value: Proactively manage the project to achieve the first significant value milestone, ensuring the customer sees a tangible return on their investment.

  • Execute a Successful Transition: Manage the project closeout and execute a smooth transition of the customer to their long-term Customer Success Manager (CSM) and Professional Services for ongoing support and expansion.

The Experience You Bring

  • 5+ years in a hands-on technical delivery role, such as implementation consulting, professional services, or solutions architecture.

  • Proven project leadership experience, with a track record of successfully delivering complex enterprise software solutions.

  • Hands-on experience with integration technologies (APIs, ESBs, iPaaS) and a deep understanding of enterprise data systems.

  • Practical knowledge of AI/ML capabilities and implementation strategies, including familiarity with agent frameworks (e.g., LangChain, Lavender, CrewAI).

  • Experience with cloud-native services and infrastructure across AWS, Azure, or GCP.

  • Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to troubleshoot technical issues during implementation.

  • Excellent customer management and communication skills,

You will not only drive the technical vision but also ensure the strategic evolution of a high-performance, scalable, and secure search infrastructure that powers mission-critical data discovery and analytics for global enterprises. You will solve the most complex, high-impact technical problems that span multiple organizational boundaries. Key Responsibilities Strategic Technical Leadership: Drive the multi-year technical roadmap and architectural vision for search services, ensuring they are secure, cost-efficient, and highly scalable for petabyte-scale enterprise workloads. Architectural Oversight: Design and oversee the implementation of large-scale OpenSearch clusters, ensuring low-latency indexing and query performance across multi-cloud environments. Deep Platform Integration: Lead the deep integration of OpenSearch with core CDP components like Apache Iceberg, SDX, and Ozone to provide a unified, secure search experience. Engineering Excellence: Define and champion organization-wide best practices for security (RBAC, TLS), observability, and enterprise-grade reliability. Complex Problem Solving: Design solutions for extreme scale, multi-tenancy isolation, and complex hybrid/multi-cloud deployment models. Mentorship & Influence: Mentor staff and senior engineers on complex technical designs and foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation across the entire engineering organization. Open Source Stewardship: Act as a primary influencer and liaison within the upstream OpenSearch community, aligning their roadmap with Cloudera's product strategy. Technical Requirements Search Expertise: 10+ years of experience (scaling from the 8+ years required for Staff level) working with OpenSearch or Elasticsearch in large-scale production environments. Distributed Systems Mastery: Deep understanding of consensus algorithms, CAP theorem, replication, sharding, and fault-tolerant design patterns. Mastery in Programming: Mastery of Java for core development and proficiency in Go or Python for advanced automation and tooling. Infrastructure & Cloud: Extensive experience with Kubernetes (K8s) and container orchestration, including deploying search workloads on AWS (EKS/AOSS), Azure (AKS), or Google Cloud (GKE). Systems Thinking: Demonstrated ability to drive architectural decisions with a focus on non-functional requirements such as high availability and security. Preferred Qualifications Deep expertise in Lucene internals (e.g., segment merging, bitsets, and codecs) Advanced knowledge of Vector Database capabilities for Generative AI (RAG) and retrieval-augmented analytics. Significant history of contributions to open-source projects like the Apache Software Foundation or the OpenSearch Project Why Cloudera? At Cloudera, we believe data can make what is impossible today, possible tomorrow. We empower people to transform complex data into clear and actionable insights. You’ll work on a platform that handles more data than almost anyone else on the planet, surrounded by a team that values candor, innovation, and open-source integrity. Cloudera is looking for a senior software engineer to join the Atlas Engineering team within SDX (Shared Data Experience) Services organization to define, drive, develop and maintain Atlas products enabling data security for Cloudera Data Platform. Apache Atlas is designed to exchange metadata with other tools and processes within and outside of the Hadoop stack, thereby enabling platform-agnostic governance controls that effectively address compliance requirements. Apache Atlas provides scalable governance for Enterprise Hadoop that is driven by metadata. Atlas, at its core, is designed to easily model new business processes and data assets with agility. https://atlas.apache.org About SDX: Data usage in enterprises today is growing both in size and complexity. There is a need for a consistent experience of data across the different usage patterns of data— be it data warehousing, machine learning, etc. Cloudera's enterprise data platform (CDP) is uniquely positioned to provide this shared data experience (SDX). https://www.cloudera.com/products/sdx.html About the team: You will be working with a team responsible for designing, building, and delivering features across Atlas products. The primary responsibility of the team is to provide high-quality Metadata management service in our CDP products (Public Cloud, Private Cloud Base and other deployments). The engineering team works on extending Apache Atlas to meet the needs of enterprise customers such as cross-data center replication, scalability, performance, security policy management, etc. This role requires a startup mindset to build features incrementally and pivot according to feedback from customers and clients. You will have to work in close collaboration with engineers from globally-distributed teams, product managers and UX designers. You must be willing to learn new things, be self-motivated, innovative and proactive. As the senior software engineer you will: Develop cross-product features to enable a shared data experience for enterprise customers. Design, code, and implement elegant, scalable, enterprise-quality software. Work iteratively in an Agile development environment Build strong relationships and collaborate with platform and UI engineers, quality engineers, and UX designers as well as with Product Management, Field Engineering, and other external partners. Work on multiple codebases to solve complex problems. Work with the support team in addressing customer issues We’re excited about you if you have : 5+ years of software engineering experience developing products and/or platforms for internal or external customers Experience with back-end platform and server technologies using at least one of these languages - Java, C++, Python. Experience with REST API, Microservices development and technologies. Experience with cloud technologies (AWS, GCE, Azure) Development experience in Linux environments. Experience with collaboration tools (e.g., Jenkins, Jira) Familiarity with additional development tools such as JUnit, Maven & Jenkins. Clean coding habits, attention to detail, and focus on quality Self-driven and motivated, with a strong sense of ownership and craftsmanship Strong written and oral communication skills and collaboration skills. Ability to understand and address complex problems and systems. Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team. Self-Starter who has worked in high-growth, SaaS and PasS Environments. You may also have: Big Data / NoSQL / distributed database experience Goo

Joining Collibra’s Strategy and Operations team

We're looking for a BI & Analytics Manager to own customer lifecycle analytics within our Analytics & Insights team, part of Collibra's Strategy function. You'll be responsible for understanding how customers adopt, expand, and succeed with Collibra's platform, and for building the analytical foundations that make those insights scalable, governed, and increasingly AI-assisted.

This role sits at the intersection of strategic analytics and the future of how Collibra consumes its own data. You'll own a critical analytics domain end-to-end, from customer health and product adoption through expansion patterns and renewal dynamics. But you'll also help shape how we govern the data and semantic models that underpin those insights, and how we move toward AI-powered self-service analytics. We're building Collibra to be its own first customer for data governance and AI-assisted discovery, and this role is part of that ambition.

You'll report to the Director, Analytics and Insights and be the dedicated analytics partner for our Services leadership, and a key contributor to the cross-functional diagnostic frameworks our executive committee relies on. You'll work within a team that's building a unified, AI-ready data foundation, partnering closely with our Data Office, cross-functional counterparts in RevOps and Product, and senior stakeholders who expect data-driven counsel, not just data delivery.

If you're energized by customer lifecycle analytics, building governed and AI-ready data products, and the opportunity to shape how a data governance company does analytics internally, we want to talk.

This is a hybrid role based in our Brussels office. Our hybrid model means you’ll work from the office at least two days each week. This setup helps us stay connected, work more closely together, and keep making progress as a team.

BI & Analytics Managers at Collibra are responsible for

  • Own customer lifecycle analytics: health scoring, adoption patterns, expansion signals, renewal dynamics, and everything in between
  • Be the analytics partner for Services leadership. Understand their priorities, shape the agenda, and bring insights before they're asked for
  • Build diagnostic frameworks that connect product usage to commercial outcomes so leadership can see where customers get stuck or succeed
  • Lead analysis from start to finish: gather data, build models, test hypotheses about customer behavior, and communicate findings clearly to stakeholders
  • Feed insights into executive reporting with context and narrative, not just numbers
  • Work with the Foundation team on the unified customer and product data model. Make sure your domain is well-represented and well-documented
  • Champion data governance best practices within the analytics team: maintain clear data documentation, metric definitions, and lineage for your domain, contributing to the semantic layer that enables AI-assisted analytics
  • Push the boundaries of our AI analytics tooling. We're already using AI-assisted analysis and we need someone who'll keep finding new ways to make it work harder for the team and for stakeholders
  • Use the Collibra platform as part of your daily workflow to document, govern, and share the data products you create. You'll help us understand our own product through the lens of a power user
  • Work with cross-functional partners in RevOps, Marketing, and Product to connect the dots across acquisition, adoption, expansion, and renewal

You have

  • 5+ years of experience in analytics, business intelligence, or a related field, with a meaningful portion spent on customer success, lifecycle, or post-sale analytics in a SaaS environment.

AI Engineer - FDE (Forward Deployed Engineer) (ALL LEVELS)

Req ID: CSQ426R237

Mission

The AI Forward Deployed Engineering (AI FDE) team is a highly specialised customer-facing AI team at Databricks. We deliver professional services engagements to help our customers build and productionize first-of-its-kind AI applications. We work cross-functionally to shape long-term strategic priorities and initiatives alongside engineering, product, and developer relations, as well as support internal subject matter expert (SME) teams. We view our team as an ensemble: we look for individuals with strong, unique specialisations to improve the overall strength of the team. This team is the right fit for you if you love working with customers, teammates, and fueling your curiosity for the latest trends in GenAI, LLMOps, and ML more broadly.

We welcome remote applicants located near our offices. The preferred locations (in priority order) are London (UK), Munich (Germany), Paris (France), and Amsterdam (NL).

The impact you will have:

  • Develop cutting-edge GenAI solutions, incorporating the latest techniques from our Mosaic AI research to solve customer problems
  • Own production rollouts of consumer and internally facing GenAI applications
  • Serve as a trusted technical advisor to customers across a variety of domains
  • Present at conferences such as Data + AI Summit, recognized as a thought leader internally and externally
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with the product and engineering teams to influence priorities and shape the product roadmap 

What we look for:

  • Experience building GenAI applications, including RAG, multi-agent systems, Text2SQL, fine-tuning, etc., with tools such as HuggingFace, LangChain, and DSPy
  • Expertise in deploying production-grade GenAI applications, including evaluation and optimizations 
  • Extensive years of hands-on industry data science experience, leveraging common machine learning and data science tools, i.e. pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch, etc.
  • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP
  • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline (Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research, etc.) or equivalent practical experience
  • Experience communicating and/or teaching technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences alike
  • Passion for collaboration, life-long learning, and driving business value through AI
  • [Preferred] Experience using the Databricks Intelligence Platform and Apache Spark™ to process large-scale distributed datasets
  • We require fluency in English and welcome candidates who also speak French or German

About Databricks

Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter,

Strong understanding of QA best practices, test planning, and Agile methodologies. Proficiency with version control systems (e.g., Git), CI/CD pipelines, and test management tools (e.g., Jira, Zephyr). Certifications in Salesforce or NetSuite platforms (e.g., Salesforce Platform Developer, NetSuite ERP Consultant). Exposure to prompt engineering or building/testing GenAI-powered features.

Customer"）を目的とします。直接ケースを多数対応するというよりも、ケース進行や優先順位付けに深く関与し、関係する全ての Snowflake チームが顧客特有の状況を理解した上で、明確な方向性のもとに対応を進められるよう支援します。また、傾向の可視化や関連課題の集約、改善提案を通じて、お客様が Snowflake 英語での基本的な口頭でのコミュニケーション能力 中級レベル以上の英語のリーディング・ライティングスキル データウェアハウス、データベース移行、ETL に関する経験 RDBMS の基本的な知識（SQL、データ型、集約、実行計画、ワークロード管理、DB性能管理） 主要クラウド（AWS, Azure, GCP）のいずれかに関する知識（インフラ、ネットワーク、性能指標） Snowflakeはデータ革命の最前線に立ち、世界最高のデータとアプリケーションのプラットフォームの構築に取り組んでいます。私たちのミッションはデータサイロ化を解消して、「世界中のデータをモビライズすること」を目指しております。急成長していくデータの未来を私たちと共に築いていきましょう！ Solutions Engineering’s Mission? 当社では、お客様の複雑な課題を解決し、大規模な案件に経験のあるシニアセールスエンジニアを募集しています。ソリューションエンジニアはセールスチームやチャネルパートナーと協業して、お客様のニーズを理解し、セールスサイクルを元に案件支援を計画して、説得力のある価値志向のデモンストレーションを提供し、お客様のPoCをサポートし、案件の成約まで支援します。Snowflakeのソリューションエンジニアとして、「データ領域を再定義する」という当社の情熱を共有し、変化の激しい環境でも積極的に自ら関与して実行する柔軟性と意欲をが求められているます。ビジネスと技術の両方において、エグゼクティブと会話、技術的な方々と話をします。 The impact You Will Make: 見込み客やお客様のエグゼクティブやIT技術者にSnowflakeのテクノロジーやビジョンのプレゼンテーションを実施 見込み客やお客様に対してデモやPoCなどの方法で営業サイクル全体を通してSnowflakeテクノロジーの価値を実証 進化し続ける業界に身を置き、競合他社や関連する技術やベンダーを理解し、Snowflakeをどのようにポジショニングするかの理解 製品管理、エンジニアリング、マーケティングと協力して、Snowflakeの製品改善やマーケティング活動を実施 Who You Are： 5～6年以上の業界経験を持ち、カスタマーフェイシングの経験を2年以上お持ちの方 ホワイトボードでの即席プレゼンテーションやデモの使用など、技術者とエグゼクティブの両方へのプレゼンテーションスキル 大規模なデータベース、データウェアハウス技術、ETL、アナリティクス、クラウドテクノロジーの幅広い経験 SQLとSQLアナリティクスのハンズオン知識 顧客の具体的なビジネス上の課題とSnowflakeのソリューションを結びつける提案スキル コンピュータサイエンス、エンジニアリング、数学、または関連分野の大卒、または同等の経験をお持ちの方 日本語と英語に堪能な方 Japan Benefits (一部) Free lunch at office 401K（企業型確定拠出年金） RSU Phone & Gym reimbursement 3 day Summer Vacation & 5 day Year-end Holidays Paid Parental Leave Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake. How do you want to make your impact? For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com

Trusted by the Fortune 2000, Syniti helps leading businesses reconfigure the role of enterprise data from afterthought to foundational first step; enabling them to unlock valuable insights that ignite growth, reduce risk, and expand their competitive advantage. Syniti’s Data First philosophy and enterprise data management platform supports data migration, data quality, data replication, data matching, master data management, analytics, data governance, and data strategy in a single, unified solution. As an innovative, global leader in Enterprise Data Management, the combination of our award-winning software platform and premier consultants creates a unique advantage for leading enterprises. Syniti is also a preferred data solution used by the world’s top system integrators . Headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts with offices in 25 countries around the world, Syniti operates in all global regions and industry verticals, and maintains a 100% client success rate across thousands of complex data projects and initiatives. THE ROLE The Network & Information Security Manager will oversee both the security posture and network architecture of Syniti’s global SaaS platform, spanning AWS, Azure, and SAP BTP environments. The role is responsible for meeting regulatory standards including FedRAMP High, IL4/IL5, CMMC 2.0 Level 2, ITAR, and UK Cyber Essentials+. This role leads security architecture reviews, incident readiness, and also serves as the technical lead for network design, implementation, and optimization. This includes responsibility for network segmentation, secure connectivity, and inter-region network architecture using technologies such as AWS Cloud WAN, AWS Transit Gateway, Azure Virtual WAN, PrivateLink, and SAP BTP interconnectivity options. As a strategic security and network leader within Cloud Operations, this role collaborates with Engineering, SRE, Compliance, and external auditors to implement and maintain zero trust principles, control enforcement, and secure network transport. WHAT YOU WILL DO Own and manage vulnerability management program across cloud and containerized workloads. Oversee threat detection, incident response, and forensic analysis coordination with external SOC provider. Own and manage network design, segmentation, and secure interconnectivity across AWS, Azure, and SAP BTP environments using services like Cloud WAN, Transit Gateway, Azure Virtual WAN, and PrivateLink. Zilliz is a fast-growing startup developing the industry’s leading vector database company for enterprise-grade AI. Founded by the engineers behind Milvus, the world’s most popular open-source vector database, the company builds next-generation database technologies to help organizations quickly create AI applications. On a mission to democratize AI, Zilliz is committed to simplifying data management for AI applications and making vector databases accessible to every organization.


Zilliz is looking for an early-career engineer to join our team as an Open Source Support Engineer and grow into a technical expert while helping developers build applications with Milvus, the leading open-source vector database.

Milvus powers vector search for thousands of developers worldwide. By helping open-source users succeed in real production environments, this role directly contributes to the growth of the Milvus ecosystem, while providing critical feedback that shapes the product’s evolution.

This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates or engineers early in their careers who are excited to solve real infrastructure problems, work directly with users, and develop deep expertise in distributed systems and vector search technology.
